##NAMES=Sperlingite
##RRUFFID=R250061
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(H_2_O)K(Mn^2+^Fe^3+^)(Al_2_Ti)(PO_4_)_4_[O(OH)][(H_2_O)_9_(OH)]·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Hagendorf-Süd pegmatite, Oberpfalz, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless prisms with pyramidal terminations in association with columbite, hopeite, leucophosphite, mitridatite, scholzite, orange–brown zincoberaunite sprays and tiny green crystals of zincolibethenite. Type sample, described in Rewitzer et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250061
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)_0.96_K_0.04_](K_0.52_[box]_0.48_)(Mn^2+^_0.60_Mg_0.33_Zn_0.29_Fe^3+^_0.77_)(Al_1.05_Ti^4+^_1.33_Fe^3+^_0.62_)(PO_4_)_4_[F_0.19_(OH)_0.94_O_0.87_][(H_2_O)_9.23_(OH)_0.77_]·3.96H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.43 b: 20.28 c: 12.22 alpha: 90 beta: 90.1 gamma: 90 volume: 2585 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
